flashcards.front: What is a class in C++?
flashcards.back: A class is a user-defined data type that serves as a blueprint for creating objects. It encapsulates data members and member functions into a single unit.

flashcards.front: Who developed C++ and in which year?
flashcards.back: C++ was developed by Bjarne Stroustrup at Bell Labs in 1979 as an extension of the C programming language.

flashcards.front: What are the three access specifiers in C++?
flashcards.back: The three access specifiers are: public (accessible everywhere), private (accessible only within the class), and protected (accessible within class and derived classes).
Encapsulation is the bundling of data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit (class), restricting direct access to some components.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a class and an object?
flashcards.back: A class is a blueprint or template that defines properties and behaviors; an object is an instance of a class with actual memory allocation.
A constructor is a special member function automatically called when an object is created. It initializes the object's data members.
A destructor is a special member function automatically called when an object goes out of scope or is deleted. It performs cleanup operations.
The 'this' pointer is an implicit pointer that points to the current object instance. It is used to access members of the current object.
A member function is a function declared inside a class definition. It has access to all class members and defines the behavior of objects.
A data member (also called instance variable) is a variable declared inside a class that stores data for each object of the class.
A friend function is a function not belonging to a class but granted access to private and protected members of that class.
Static data members are class variables shared by all objects of the class. They are initialized once and exist regardless of object instantiation.

flashcards.front: What is the default access specifier for class members?
flashcards.back: The default access specifier for class members in C++ is private.

flashcards.front: How do you define a class object in C++?
flashcards.back: A class object is defined by specifying the class name followed by the object name, e.g., ClassName objectName; Similar to declaring variables.

flashcards.front: What is the scope resolution operator (::) used for in classes?
flashcards.back: The scope resolution operator is used to define member functions outside the class definition and to access static members.
